name: meta
summary: Register a post meta key on a post type via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a class that calls register_post_meta() on Ready, exposing the meta to the REST API and applying type/default/sanitize callbacks.
problem: You need a piece of structured data attached to posts of a specific post type — and you want WordPress to know the type, default, and visibility so the REST API exposes it correctly.
appliesWhen: You need a single meta key on a known post type
You want REST API exposure for the meta
Other code reads/writes the meta via get_post_meta/update_post_meta directly
avoidWhen: You want a typed accessor (use phpnomad/meta-provider, or phpnomad/attribute for both)
The meta is a one-off internal flag with no REST exposure (just call get_post_meta directly)
You need term/user/comment meta — this recipe targets post meta only
examples: Register a price meta key on the product post type
Add an event_date meta to events
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. The meta lives at the WordPress level only; consumers must use get_post_meta/update_post_meta.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that registers the meta on Ready
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit defaultValue() to return the actual default
Customize sanitize_callback and auth_callback if the meta is user-editable

name.description: Class name for the registrar (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: PascalCase. Convention: name after the meta concept (EventDate), not RegisterEventDate.

metaKey.description: Meta key stored in the database
metaKey.aiHint: Convention: prefix with _ to hide from default custom-fields UI, plus plugin slug to avoid collisions.

postType.description: Post type slug the meta attaches to

metaType.description: WordPress meta type: string, boolean, integer, number, array, object
metaType.aiHint: One of WordPress's allowed types: string, boolean, integer, number, array, object.

description.description: Human description shown in REST schemas
description.example: Event start date in ISO 8601 format

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener in
